plan: We will trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
All leveraged ETFs like SOXL and SPAL will be sold to eliminate high-risk exposure.
QQQI will be increased to 25% for more income and broad index stability.
SMH and QQQ will be trimmed to reduce exposure to downtrending positions.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio leverage.
A significant portion of the book will be held in cash after these trims.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdown, but also limited upside potential. The primary risk is underperformance in a strong bull market.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to 25% to adhere to concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
SOXL and SPAL will be sold to eliminate high-risk leveraged positions.
QQQI and SMH will be trimmed to reduce exposure to their current downtrends.
We will buy ZS at 10% to add a high-scoring, quality software name.
This strategy blends broad index exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage.
A 10% cash buffer will be maintained for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It aims for a blend of stability and moderate upside. Expect moderate volatility; significant market downturns could still lead to notable drawdowns.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to 25% to meet concentration limits.
QQQI, QQQ, and SMH will be trimmed to reduce exposure to downtrending assets.
SOXL will be added to 10% to lean into its strong scanner read, despite its current P&L.
ZS will be bought at 15% to concentrate in a top-scoring software setup.
NVDA will be bought at 10% to add another high-scoring chip name.
This plan uses leverage and concentration for aggressive growth potential.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month by leveraging high-scoring setups and using leveraged ETFs. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential for large drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, amplifying losses if not managed strictly with stops.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital.
All leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, will be sold to eliminate high-beta risk.
CRWD will be trimmed and SMH will be sold to reduce exposure to high-beta names and sectors.
QQQI will be significantly increased to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
A substantial cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
income.read: This income-focused approach aims for ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. While offering cash-like income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. Expect very low volatility but also limited capital appreciation.
